feature_name: Fix README install one-liners: sudo-free install path and inline comments

completed_tasks.title: QA remediation: Linux PATH fix, macOS mkdir -p, whitespace alignment
completed_tasks.description: Three fixes from QA review: (1) In the Linux block, change the final `namer` invocation on the closing line from bare `namer` to `~/.local/bin/namer` so it works even when ~/.local/bin is not yet in PATH for the current session. (2) In BOTH macOS blocks (Apple Silicon and Intel), add `sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/bin &&` as the first command inside the subshell (after the skip-if-installed line), with a comment `# Ensure install directory exists`. This fixes a real bug: on a fresh Apple Silicon Mac, /usr/local/bin may not exist. (3) Align trailing comment spacing consistently across all three Unix blocks so the `#` comments start at the same column where feasible.

completed_tasks.title: Replace three Unix install blocks and update introductory prose in README.md
completed_tasks.description: In README.md, replace the three single-line Unix install commands (macOS Apple Silicon, macOS Intel, Linux x86-64) with the multi-line commented shell blocks specified in the Design section of the exec plan. Update the introductory prose from 'one-liner' to 'snippet'. Verify with bash -n that each block is syntactically valid shell. Confirm Windows and build-from-source blocks are untouched. Run npx markdownlint-cli2 '**/*.md' and fix any lint errors.

history.note: Feature initiated: fix README install one-liners to ensure /usr/local/bin exists before download, and add inline comments explaining each step for all platforms
Moved to discovery. PM to finalize install path decision, gather requirements, and write exec plan.
Discovery complete. Exec plan written. Install path decision: ~/.local/bin (sudo-free). Acceptance criteria defined. Awaiting /design.
Moved to design. PE to produce design section in exec plan. Docs-only change to README.md.
Moved to tasks. Single task created: replace three Unix install blocks and update intro prose in README.md.
Moved to implementation. Task 1: replace three Unix install blocks and update intro prose in README.md.

Task 1 complete. README.md: replaced macOS Apple Silicon, macOS Intel, and Linux x86-64 install blocks with multi-line commented snippets (sudo-free path on Linux via ~/.local/bin; sudo with explanation on macOS). Updated intro prose 'one-liner' -> 'snippet'. Windows and build-from-source blocks untouched. bash -n passed on all three blocks; markdownlint 0 errors.
Moved to review. QA to review README.md shell snippets for correctness on fresh macOS/Linux, verify mkdir -p fix for macOS /usr/local/bin handling, and check standards compliance.

QA returned REQUEST CHANGES with 3 fixes. Created task 2 for remediation: (1) Linux final invocation must use full path ~/.local/bin/namer, (2) macOS blocks need sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/bin back, (3) align trailing comment whitespace across all three blocks.

Task 2 complete. README.md: (1) Linux closing line changed from `namer` to `~/.local/bin/namer`. (2) Both macOS blocks gained `sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/bin &&    # Ensure install directory exists` as first command inside subshell. (3) Trailing comment whitespace aligned to consistent column across all three Unix blocks. bash -n passed on all three blocks; markdownlint 0 errors.

Moved to re-review. QA to verify three fixes from previous review are correctly applied: (1) Linux full path ~/.local/bin/namer on final invocation, (2) macOS sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/bin present, (3) whitespace alignment consistent.
All tasks complete (2/2). Moved to acceptance. PM to validate every acceptance criterion against current README.md.

PM rejected AC-1 and AC-2 because exec plan said 'no mkdir -p on macOS'. Design assumption was wrong: /usr/local/bin is NOT guaranteed on fresh macOS (especially Apple Silicon). Corrected exec plan: R1, AC-1, AC-2 now REQUIRE sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/bin. Design section and exact blocks updated. Decision log entry added. Re-running acceptance with corrected criteria.
Feature complete. All 8 acceptance criteria verified PASS (manual validation). Exec plan moved to docs/exec-plans/completed/. No tech debt created.
